name: Belizean Fry Jacks with Refried Beans
steps: Mix the flour and baking powder with a splash of water into a soft dough; rest 10 minutes.
Roll into a round and fry in hot oil until puffed and golden on both sides.
Serve with the warmed, mashed pinto beans alongside.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour and baking powder with a pinch of salt, then add water a little at a time to form a soft, slightly sticky dough. Cover and rest 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile, saute the onion until soft, add the pinto beans with a splash of water, and mash roughly, cooking until thick.
Divide the dough into two, roll each piece into a thin round, and cut a small slit in the center of each.
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Fry each round 1-2 minutes per side until puffed and deep golden.
Drain briefly on a paper towel, then serve warm alongside the refried beans, crumbling the queso fresco over the beans.

name: Belizean Johnny Cakes
steps: Mix the flour, butter, coconut milk, and baking powder into a soft dough.
Shape into small rounds and bake at 375F for 18-22 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, sugar, butter, and coconut milk into a soft, slightly sticky dough. Knead briefly.
Shape into small rounds, about 2 inches across.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 20-25 minutes until golden - Belizean johnny cakes are a distinctly coconut-enriched biscuit, eaten warm with butter or jam for breakfast.

name: Belizean Scrambled Eggs with Fry Jacks
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into triangles, and fry until puffed and golden.
Scramble the eggs.
Serve together.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and warm water into a soft dough. Rest 15 minutes, then roll out and cut into triangles.
Fry the triangles in the oil, 2-3 minutes per side, until puffed and golden.
Scramble the eggs in the butter, and serve together - a classic, hearty Belizean breakfast.

name: Belizean Bollos
steps: Mix the masa harina with the coconut milk and sugar into a soft dough.
Wrap small portions in foil and steam 30-35 minutes until firm.
steps_detailed: Mix the masa harina with the coconut milk, sugar, and cinnamon into a soft, cohesive dough.
Wrap small portions tightly in foil (traditionally banana leaf) and steam 32-40 minutes until firm.
Bollos, sweet corn dumplings, are a simple, traditional Belizean breakfast.

name: Belizean Rice and Beans
steps: Simmer the rice with the beans, coconut milk, and garlic until the rice is tender, about 20-25 minutes.
Serve as a hearty side or light meal.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the rice, beans, coconut milk, paprika, and 3/4 cup water, and simmer, covered, 22-28 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Belizean rice and beans, rich with coconut milk, is a beloved Sunday staple, distinct from the everyday version made without coconut.

name: Belizean Garnaches

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps: Fry the tortilla until crisp.
Spread with the refried beans and top with the queso fresco and cabbage.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps_detailed: Fry the tortilla in a little oil until crisp and flat.
Spread generously with the refried beans, then top with the crumbled queso fresco, shredded cabbage, and diced tomatoes.
Garnaches are a beloved Belizean snack-lunch, similar dishes found with different names across neighboring countries.

name: Belizean Escabeche
steps: Simmer the chicken in water until tender, about 25 minutes.
Pickle the onion and jalapeno in the vinegar while the chicken cooks.
Serve the chicken in broth topped with the pickled onions.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the garlic and paprika in water to cover for 25-30 minutes until tender.
Toss the sliced onion and jalapeno with the vinegar and let pickle while the chicken simmers, at least 20 minutes.
Serve the chicken in its broth, piled high with the tangy pickled onions - escabeche, a distinctly Belizean onion soup, is a beloved comfort dish.

name: Belizean Conch Soup
steps: Simmer the sweet potato and onion in the coconut milk with a splash of water for 10 minutes.
Add the shrimp and simmer 5-6 minutes until just cooked through.
Serve hot.
steps_detailed: Simmer the sweet potato, onion, and garlic in the coconut milk with 1/2 cup water for 12-15 minutes until the sweet potato is nearly tender.
Add the shrimp (standing in for conch, a prized and now carefully regulated Belizean shellfish) and simmer gently 5-7 minutes until just cooked through.
Top with cilantro and serve hot - conch soup is a treasured Belizean coastal specialty, especially during conch season.

name: Belizean Stewed Chicken
steps: Rub the chicken with the achiote paste and garlic.
Brown well, then add a splash of water and the brown sugar, and simmer 25-30 minutes until tender.
Serve with rice and beans.
steps_detailed: Rub the chicken thoroughly with the achiote paste, garlic, and lime juice, and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Brown the chicken well with the onion, then add the brown sugar and a splash of water.
Cover and simmer 28-35 minutes until deeply tender, the sauce rich and reddish from the achiote.
Stewed chicken is considered Belize's national dish, always paired with rice and beans.

name: Belizean Hudut
steps: Simmer the fish in the coconut milk with the garlic for 10-12 minutes until cooked through.
Mash the plantain into a smooth side.
Serve the fish and coconut broth over the mashed plantain.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, garlic, and jalapeno briefly, then add the coconut milk and simmer 5 minutes.
Add the fish and poach gently 10-14 minutes until just cooked through.
Mash the plantain until smooth.
Serve the fish and rich coconut broth ladled over the mashed plantain - hudut is a beloved Garifuna dish, central to Belize's Afro-Indigenous coastal culture.

name: Belizean Sere
steps: Simmer the sweet potato and onion in the coconut milk for 10 minutes.
Add the fish and simmer 10-12 more minutes until cooked through.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Simmer the sweet potato, onion, and garlic in the coconut milk with a splash of water for 12-15 minutes until the sweet potato is nearly tender.
Add the fish and simmer gently 10-14 more minutes until just cooked through and flaky.
Top with cilantro and serve with rice - sere, a Garifuna coconut fish soup, is a beloved coastal Belizean dish.

name: Belizean Boil-Up
steps: Simmer the potato and plantain in water until tender, about 15-18 minutes.
Add the fish and simmer 8-10 more minutes until cooked through.
Serve everything together with the hard-boiled egg and a tomato-onion sauce if you have one.
steps_detailed: Simmer the potato, cassava, and plantain together in water for 18-22 minutes until tender.
Add the fish and simmer 10-12 more minutes until cooked through.
Warm the diced tomatoes into a simple sauce.

Serve everything together on one plate with the hard-boiled egg and tomato sauce spooned over - boil-up, a hearty one-pot combination of root vegetables, fish, and eggs, is widely considered one of Belize's most iconic dishes.

name: Belizean Panades
steps: Make a simple dough from the masa harina and water, and press into small rounds.
Fill with the flaked fish, fold closed, and fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Make a soft dough from the masa harina and warm water, and press into small flat rounds.
Mix the flaked fish with the onion and jalapeno, and place a spoonful in the center of each round, folding closed and sealing well.
Fry in the oil, turning, 4-5 minutes until deeply golden and crisp - panades are a beloved Belizean street snack.

name: Belizean Salbutes

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour

steps: Fry the tortilla until puffed and golden.
Top with the shredded chicken, cabbage, and tomatoes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour

steps_detailed: Fry the tortilla in hot oil until it puffs up and turns golden, 1-2 minutes.
Top generously with the shredded chicken, cabbage, tomatoes, and a squeeze of lime.
Salbutes, light and puffy, are a beloved Belizean fried-tortilla snack.

name: Belizean Cassava Chips
steps: Slice the cassava as thin as possible.
Fry in the oil until golden and crisp, in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
steps_detailed: Slice the cassava as thin and evenly as possible.
Fry in batches in the hot oil, 4-5 minutes per batch, until deeply golden and crisp.
Finish with a squeeze of lime - cassava chips are a popular, simple Belizean snack.

name: Belizean Cassava Pudding
steps: Mix the grated cassava with the coconut milk, sugar, and cinnamon.
Pour into a small buttered dish and bake at 350F for 40-45 minutes until set and golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Mix the grated cassava with the coconut milk, sugar, cinnamon, and raisins until well combined.
Pour into a small buttered baking dish.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 42-50 minutes until set, golden, and slightly firm to the touch.
Cassava pudding, dense and coconut-rich, is a treasured Belizean holiday dessert.

name: Belizean Coconut Tablet
steps: Simmer the coconut with the brown sugar and cinnamon for 8-10 minutes until thick and sticky.
Press into a small dish and chill before cutting into squares.
steps_detailed: Simmer the coconut with the brown sugar, cinnamon, and allspice over medium-low heat, stirring frequently, 10-12 minutes until thick and sticky.
Press firmly into a small lined dish and chill at least 1 hour until firm.
Cut into squares - coconut tablet, a simple, dense sweet, is beloved throughout Belize.

name: Belizean Fish Ceviche
steps: Marinate the cubed fish in the lime juice for at least 20 minutes, until opaque and firm.
Stir in the onion and jalapeno.
Serve chilled with tortilla chips.
steps_detailed: Cube the fish and marinate in the lime juice for at least 25-30 minutes, turning occasionally, until the fish turns opaque and firm.
Stir in the onion, jalapeno, cilantro, and tomatoes.
Chill briefly and serve with tortilla chips - ceviche is a beloved coastal Belizean specialty, reflecting the country's rich seafood tradition.

name: Belizean Habanero Pepper Sauce
steps: Simmer the jalapeno, onion, vinegar, and garlic for 8-10 minutes until softened.
Blend until smooth.
Serve alongside grilled meats and stewed chicken.
steps_detailed: Simmer the jalapeno, onion, vinegar, garlic, and mango over medium-low heat for 10-12 minutes until softened.
Blend until smooth, adding a splash of water if needed.
This nods to Belize's beloved fiery, fruity habanero-based pepper sauces, a condiment found on every table.

name: Belizean Coconut Rice Sauce
steps: Warm the coconut milk with the garlic and paprika for 3-4 minutes.
Serve drizzled over rice or grilled fish.
steps_detailed: Warm the coconut milk with the garlic and paprika over low heat, 4-5 minutes, until fragrant.
Stir in the lime juice off the heat.
Drizzle over rice, fish, or vegetables for a simple coconut-forward finish.
